schools.summary: Gill: these nations are afterward mentioned.
Keil & Delitzsch: the LORD leaving them to prove Israel, even as many as had not known the wars of Canaan.

themes: these are the nations which the LORD left, to prove Israel by them
God overruling his people's failure to a purpose of testing
that the trial of your faith... might be found unto praise (1 Pet. 1:7)

schools.summary: Gill: the nations were left that the young generations might by their wars learn the art of war and be inured to martial discipline.

themes: that the generations of the children of Israel might know, to teach them war
God keeping his people in the school of spiritual warfare
fight the good fight of faith (1 Tim. 6:12); put on the whole armour of God (Eph. 6:11)

schools.summary: Gill: the five Philistine lordships of Gaza, Ashdod, Ashkelon, Gath, and Ekron; the Sidonians and Hivites of Lebanon among them.

themes: five lords of the Philistines, and all the Canaanites, and the Sidonians, and the Hivites that dwelt in mount Lebanon
the foes that should have been driven out left as a standing trial
they shall be thorns in your sides (Judg. 2:3)

schools.summary: Gill: left to inure Israel to war, try their courage, and prove their faithfulness to God.
Keil & Delitzsch: them proving whether Israel would hearken to the commandments.

themes: they were to prove Israel by them, to know whether they would hearken unto the commandments of the LORD
God proving his people to make manifest the heart
the LORD your God proveth you, to know whether ye love the LORD (Deut. 13:3)

schools.summary: Gill: they dwelt among them as if only sojourners, not conquerors, by sufferance and not as owners, through their sloth and indolence.

themes: the children of Israel dwelt among the Canaanites, Hittites, and Amorites, and Perizzites, and Hivites, and Jebusites
the peril of God's people making their home among the ungodly
come out from among them, and be ye separate (2 Cor. 6:17)

schools.summary: Gill: they intermarried contrary to God's express command, confounding their families, debasing their blood, and being ensnared into idolatry.

themes: they took their daughters to be their wives, and gave their daughters to their sons, and served their gods
the forbidden union leading straight to idolatry
be ye not unequally yoked together with unbelievers (2 Cor. 6:14-15)

schools.summary: Gill: they did evil both by marrying with heathens and worshipping their gods.
Keil & Delitzsch: this the first apostasy bringing chastisement.

themes: the children of Israel did evil in the sight of the LORD, and forgat the LORD their God, and served Baalim and the groves
the forgetting of God the root of all apostasy
they soon forgat his works... and served their idols: which were a snare (Ps. 106:13, 36)

schools.summary: Gill: the Lord's anger was hot because of their idolatry.
Keil & Delitzsch: the LORD selling them into the hand of the king of Mesopotamia.

themes: the anger of the LORD was hot against Israel, and he sold them into the hand of Chushanrishathaim... eight years
apostasy provoking God's anger and bringing servitude
he delivered them into the hands of spoilers that spoiled them (Judg. 2:14)

schools.summary: Gill: they cried toward the close of the eight years' bondage, groaning under their oppression.
Clarke: the LORD raising up a deliverer, Othniel.

themes: when the children of Israel cried unto the LORD, the LORD raised up a deliverer... even Othniel
God hearing the cry of his chastened people and sending salvation
they cried unto the LORD in their trouble, and he saved them (Ps. 107:13)

schools.summary: Gill: the Spirit moved him to the work, inspired him with courage, and filled him with every needful gift.

themes: the Spirit of the LORD came upon him, and he judged Israel... and the LORD delivered Chushanrishathaim... into his hand
all true deliverance flowing from the Spirit's enabling
the Spirit of the LORD shall rest upon him (Isa. 11:2); to preach deliverance to the captives (Luke 4:18)

schools.summary: Gill: the forty years' rest from the time of this deliverance, by some reckoned to include the eight years' servitude; Othniel died.

themes: And the land had rest forty years. And Othniel the son of Kenaz died
the peace of restoration, yet bound to the mortal deliverer's life
he ever liveth to make intercession for them (Heb. 7:25)

schools.summary: Gill: they fell into idolatry again, a great evil and what they were prone to.
Henry: introduces Ehud, the next of the judges.

themes: the children of Israel did evil again... and the LORD strengthened Eglon the king of Moab against Israel
God using even the oppressor as his rod against his sinning people
O Assyrian, the rod of mine anger (Isa. 10:5)

schools.summary: Gill: the Lord inclined them, or the king of Moab got them to join, being his neighbours, in the war against Israel; he possessed the city of palm trees.

themes: he gathered unto him the children of Ammon and Amalek, and went and smote Israel, and possessed the city of palm trees
the foes of God's people combining when his hand is withdrawn
the LORD sold them into the hands of their enemies round about (Judg. 2:14)

schools.summary: Gill: ten years longer than they served the king of Mesopotamia, a severer correction for their relapse.
Ellicott: an instance of their bondage.

themes: So the children of Israel served Eglon the king of Moab eighteen years
a severer chastisement for relapse after mercy
if any man draw back, my soul shall have no pleasure in him (Heb. 10:38)

schools.summary: Gill: they cried after long oppression, brought to a sense of their sins and humiliation; Ehud was a left-handed man of Benjamin.

themes: the LORD raised them up a deliverer, Ehud the son of Gera, a Benjamite, a man lefthanded
God using the unlikely, the weak made strong for his work
God hath chosen the weak things of the world to confound the... mighty (1 Cor. 1:27)

schools.summary: Gill: a little sword with two edges, about half a yard long, which he could conceal, being left-handed; girt on his right thigh.

themes: Ehud made him a dagger which had two edges, of a cubit length; and he did gird it under his raiment upon his right thigh
the deliverer prepared and resolute, the two-edged blade
the word of God is... sharper than any twoedged sword (Heb. 4:12)

schools.summary: Gill: he was accompanied by servants who bore the presents, as became a judge of Israel; Eglon was a very fat man.

themes: And he brought the present unto Eglon king of Moab: and Eglon was a very fat man
the prosperity of the wicked no security, ripe for judgment
pull them out like sheep for the slaughter, and prepare them for the day of slaughter (Jer. 12:3)

schools.summary: Gill: he had delivered the things and very probably made a speech to the king in the name of Israel.
Keil & Delitzsch: him sending away the people that bore the present.

themes: when he had made an end to offer the present, he sent away the people that bare the present
wisdom in ordering his approach to accomplish the work
a prudent man foreseeth the evil, and hideth himself (Prov. 22:3)

schools.summary: Gill: the quarries by Gilgal were places where stones were dug and hewn; the king said, Keep silence, and all went out.

themes: I have a secret errand unto thee, O king: who said, Keep silence. And all that stood by him went out from him
God turning the wishes of the wicked to serve his purpose
he taketh the wise in their own craftiness (Job 5:13)

schools.summary: Gill: Eglon had retired into his summer parlour, smaller and more private, where he used to be alone; Ehud said, I have a message from God unto thee.

themes: I have a message from God unto thee. And he arose out of his seat
the word of God commanding reverence even from the ungodly
the word of God... is a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart (Heb. 4:12)

schools.summary: Gill: being left-handed, he could the better do this without the king suspecting, who would have no fear at the moving of his left hand; he thrust it into his belly.

themes: Ehud put forth his left hand, and took the dagger from his right thigh, and thrust it into his belly
God bringing down the proud oppressor by an unlikely instrument
he shall bring upon them their own iniquity (Ps. 94:23)

schools.summary: Gill: the handle as well as the blade went in, so strong and violent was the thrust, he determining to do his business effectually.
Keil & Delitzsch: the haft going in after the blade.

themes: the haft also went in after the blade; and the fat closed upon the blade
God's judgment complete and sure, done effectually
the prosperity of fools shall destroy them (Prov. 1:32)

schools.summary: Gill: the porch, a place with many seats where people waited or the guards sat; he shut and locked the doors of the parlour.

themes: Then Ehud went forth through the porch, and shut the doors of the parlour upon him, and locked them
calm presence of mind and wisdom in the hour of danger
God hath given us the spirit of... a sound mind (2 Tim. 1:7)

schools.summary: Gill: the servants came to the parlour door to reassume their place and finish their business with the king; they supposed he covered his feet.

themes: the doors of the parlour were locked, they said, Surely he covereth his feet in his summer chamber
God ordering even men's small delays to further his purpose
the king's heart is in the hand of the LORD... he turneth it whithersoever he will (Prov. 21:1)

schools.summary: Gill: they tarried, not knowing what to think, or the meaning of the doors kept locked so long.
Keil & Delitzsch: them finding their lord fallen dead.

themes: they took a key, and opened them: and, behold, their lord was fallen down dead on the earth
the proud oppressor brought low in a moment by God's hand
how are they brought into desolation, as in a moment! (Ps. 73:19)

schools.summary: Gill: the tarrying of the servants gave Ehud time to make his escape, out of reach of pursuers; he passed beyond the quarries and escaped to Seirath.

themes: And Ehud escaped while they tarried, and passed beyond the quarries, and escaped unto Seirath
God preserving those who do his will, covering their flight
the LORD shall preserve thy going out and thy coming in (Ps. 121:8)

schools.summary: Gill: he was come to Seirath in the tribe of Ephraim.
Keil & Delitzsch: him blowing a trumpet, and Israel going down with him.

themes: he blew a trumpet in the mountain of Ephraim, and the children of Israel went down with him... and he before them
God's deliverance stirring his people to rise and follow
blow ye the trumpet in Zion... gather the people (Joel 2:15-16)

schools.summary: Gill: he said this to encourage them, putting himself at their head, ready to expose his own life.
Clarke: they took the fords of Jordan.

themes: Follow after me: for the LORD hath delivered your enemies the Moabites into your hand... and took the fords of Jordan
the leader going before in faith, the victory claimed beforehand
if God be for us, who can be against us? (Rom. 8:31)

schools.summary: Gill: these were sent into Israel to keep it in subjection, or had settled there for their convenience and profit; there escaped not a man.

themes: they slew of Moab at that time about ten thousand men, all lusty, and all men of valour; and there escaped not a man
the complete victory God gives over the mightiest foe
the strength and valour of the foe availing nothing against his hand

schools.summary: Gill: the Moabites in the land of Israel were broken, so weakened they could not bring Israel into subjection again for a long time; the land had rest eighty years.

themes: So Moab was subdued that day under the hand of Israel. And the land had rest fourscore years
the abundance of God's mercy, a long and rich peace
great peace have they which love thy law (Ps. 119:165)

schools.summary: Gill: after the death of Ehud, when Israel was in distress again, Shamgar was raised up of God to be a judge and deliverer; he slew six hundred Philistines with an ox goad.

themes: Shamgar the son of Anath, which slew of the Philistines six hundred men with an ox goad: and he also delivered Israel
God working great deliverance by the humblest of means
there is no restraint to the LORD to save by many or by few (1 Sam. 14:6)
